# ha-frontend 20260624.1 — LCARS Theme Impact Analysis

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Release tag | `20260624.1` |
| Type | Pre-release / Beta |
| Published | 2026-06-25T14:17:26Z |
| Analyzed | 2026-06-25T18:47:41Z |
| Changed files | 30 |

## Release Notes

- Show warning when priming will not work for condition (@bramkragten - #52709)
- Show supported frequencies column in radio frequency devices list (@balloob - #52851)
- Use the Jinja block comment for toggle-comment in templates (@frenck - #52854)
- Add demo voice assistants and exposed entities (@synesthesiam - #52855)
- Keep self-closing slashes when minifying svg`` templates (@piitaya - #52857)
- Use choose selector for legacy trigger fields (@frenck - #52859)
- Show action labels instead of timestamps in the logbook (@piitaya - #52861)
- Fix logbook padding and margin (@piitaya - #52864)
- Show dash for unavailable number entity in slider row (@piitaya - #52866)
- Group Sankey flow siblings under their parent to fix segment crossovers (@MindFreeze - #52867)
- Localize "(default)" label in Edit sidebar dialog (@Copilot - #52868)

---

## HIGH IMPACT

No high-impact breaking changes were identified in this release. No CSS custom properties were renamed or removed, no web component element names were changed, no shadow DOM structures targeted by ha-lcars card-mod rules were restructured, and no LCARS-targeted selectors were invalidated.

---

## MEDIUM IMPACT

### Component: `hui-logbook-card` (`src/panels/lovelace/cards/hui-logbook-card.ts`)

**What changed:** The logbook card's internal layout received padding and margin fixes (`Fix logbook padding and margin` — PR #52864). The diff shows removal of `mdiChevronRight` icon import and `navigate` import, indicating the card no longer renders a `ha-icon-button` with a chevron-right navigation button inside its shadow DOM. A new import (`ha-icon-button` replaced by something else — the diff is truncated) suggests a structural change to the card's internal toolbar or header row.

**Affected ha-lcars rules:** The ha-lcars theme does not have a direct card-mod rule targeting `hui-logbook-card$` internals by class or element name. However, the general `card-mod-card-yaml` (`:host` / `ha-card` rules) applies to all Lovelace cards including the logbook card. The padding/margin fix may change the rendered height or internal spacing of the logbook card, which could affect how LCARS header/footer border classes (`.header-left`, `.footer-right`, etc.) visually align when the logbook card is used inside an LCARS frame.

**Specific concern:** If the logbook card previously had padding that LCARS implicitly relied on for alignment within `> :not(style,card-mod,h1)` child selectors, the new padding values could cause slight misalignment of content within LCARS-framed cards.

**Suggested fix:** Visually verify logbook cards used inside LCARS `.header-*`, `.middle-*`, or `.footer-*` frame classes. If spacing is off, add an explicit card-mod rule:
```yaml
"hui-logbook-card$": |
  :host {
    padding: 0px;
    margin: 0px;
  }
```
or adjust `padding-top: 6px` on the `> :not(style,card-mod,h1)` child rule in `card-mod-card-yaml` for logbook card contexts.

---

### Component: `hui-number-entity-row` (`src/panels/lovelace/entity-rows/hui-number-entity-row.ts`)

**What changed:** The number entity row now renders a dash (`—`) for entities in both `UNAVAILABLE` **and** `UNKNOWN` states (PR #52866). Previously only `UNAVAILABLE` triggered the dash; `UNKNOWN` would have fallen through to `hass.formatEntityState()`. The change is in the `<span class="state">` render path.

**Affected ha-lcars rules:** The ha-lcars theme does not directly target `hui-number-entity-row` or its `.state` span in any card-mod rule. However, `--state-unknown-color: var(--lcars-starlight)` is set in the base variables. The visual rendering of the state span (now a literal dash string rather than a formatted state value) means the span will always contain text when the entity is unknown, which may affect width calculations in LCARS button-style cards (`.button-small`, `.button-barrel-left`, etc.) that rely on `<span class="state">` positioning:

```yaml
# In card-mod-card-yaml:
> span.state {
  text-align: right;
  width: 100%;
  padding: 6px;
  padding-bottom: 22px;
  margin: unset;
  position: absolute;
  bottom: 0;
}
```

**Suggested fix:** No immediate CSS fix required — the dash character is a single character and unlikely to cause overflow. Monitor visually in LCARS button cards hosting number entities that may be in `unknown` state. No code change needed unless overflow is observed.

---

## LOW IMPACT

### Component: `src/resources/codemirror.ts` — Jinja2 CodeMirror Editor

**What changed:** The `jinja2` language export in `langs` is now an array `[_yamlWithJinja, _jinjaCommentTokens]` instead of a single `_yamlWithJinja` extension (PR #52854). This changes the toggle-comment behavior (Ctrl+/) in the template editor to use `{# #}` block comments instead of `#` line comments.

**Affected ha-lcars rules:** The ha-lcars theme sets `code-editor-background-color: black` as a base variable. No card-mod rules target CodeMirror internals (`.cm-editor`, `.cm-content`, etc.). This is purely a behavior change in the template editor with no visual CSS impact.

**Suggested fix:** No fix needed. Optionally, the theme could style the new `{# #}` comment token class (`.tok-comment` or similar) via a CodeMirror-targeted card-mod rule if LCARS comment color theming is desired in the future.

---

### Component: `src/resources/echarts/components/sankey/sankey-layout.ts` — Sankey Chart Layout

**What changed:** A new `dominantNeighborIndex()` function and significant layout algorithm changes (sibling grouping under parent nodes) were added to the Sankey chart layout engine (PR #52867). This affects how Sankey flow chart segments are positioned to reduce crossovers.

**Affected ha-lcars rules:** The ha-lcars theme has no card-mod rules targeting ECharts Sankey chart internals. The Sankey chart renders on a `<canvas>` element, which is not styleable via CSS custom properties or shadow DOM injection.

**Suggested fix:** No fix needed. The visual output of Sankey charts will change (improved layout), but this is unrelated to theme CSS.

---

### Component: `dialog-edit-sidebar.ts` — Edit Sidebar Dialog

**What changed:** The `(default)` label in the Edit Sidebar dialog is now localized (PR #52868). This is a string/translation change only.

**Affected ha-lcars rules:** No ha-lcars card-mod rules target `dialog-edit-sidebar` specifically. The general `card-mod-dialog-yaml` applies, which sets `--lcars-primary-text`, font family, and background colors at the dialog host level. No structural change occurred.

**Suggested fix:** No fix needed.

---
